message:
fix(ci): fix proptest_time assertion and e2e timeout issues

- proptest_time: Change assertion for "tomorrow" test to accept 0-48
  hours instead of 12-48 hours. The "tomorrow 9 AM" parsing can be less
  than 12 hours away if the test runs late at night (e.g., 10 PM).

- e2e.sh: Increase default timeout from 60s to 180s to account for
  compilation time. Also run tests in --release mode to match the
  binary that's pre-built, avoiding redundant debug compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@ QUICK_TESTS=(
 JSON_OUTPUT=0
 VERBOSE=0
 FILTER=""
-TIMEOUT="${E2E_TIMEOUT:-60}"
+TIMEOUT="${E2E_TIMEOUT:-180}"
 
 log() {
     if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
@@ -119,15 +119,15 @@ for test in "${QUICK_TESTS[@]}"; do
     TEST_START=$(date +%s.%N)
 
     if [[ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]]; then
-        if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --test "$test" -- --nocapture 2>&1; then
+        if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --release --test "$test" -- --nocapture 2>&1; then
             RESULT="pass"
             ((PASSED++))
         else
             RESULT="fail"
             ((FAILED++))
         fi
     else
-        if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --test "$test" -- --nocapture >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --release --test "$test" -- --nocapture >/dev/null 2>&1; then
             RESULT="pass"
             ((PASSED++))
         else
